original_prompt: Please create a new 1 hour long event 'Follow-up meeting' on 2024-05-19 at 10:00 or at 16:00 if at 10:00 I already have something. The description should be 'Follow-up meeting to discuss the project.'.
assessment_reasoning: The user's prompt is moderately ambiguous as it specifies the event name, date, and description clearly but leaves some execution details open, particularly regarding the decision-making process for selecting the event time. The agent must infer that it should check for existing events at the specified time before determining the start time, which is not explicitly stated in the prompt.

variations.L1: Can you set up a meeting sometime on May 19, 2024?
variations.L2: Please schedule a meeting called 'Follow-up meeting' on May 19, 2024, but I need it to be flexible depending on my schedule.
variations.L3: Create a 'Follow-up meeting' on May 19, 2024, at either 10:00 or 16:00, depending on my availability. The meeting should last 1 hour and be about the project.
variations.L4: Please create a 1-hour event titled 'Follow-up meeting' on May 19, 2024, at 10:00, unless I have a conflict, in which case it should be at 16:00. The description should mention discussing the project.
variations.L5: Schedule a new calendar event named 'Follow-up meeting' for May 19, 2024. Set it for 10:00 if there are no existing events at that time; otherwise, set it for 16:00. The event should last 1 hour and include the description 'Follow-up meeting to discuss the project.'.
